addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Now I have everything I need. Note the bitmap at the bottom shows two closed paces: D (imageops-broaden) and E (imageops-precheck). Let me compose the report.

# Heat ₣BA Groom Report

## Heat header
- **Silks**: `rbk-11-mvp-parallel-cleanups`
- **Firemark**: ₣BA
- **Status**: racing (created 260426)
- **Progress**: 2 of 9 paces closed (₢BAAAD, ₢BAAAE wrapped 260426); 7 remaining

## Paddock summary
Sibling heat to ₣A_ (`rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen`) holding the post-AAK paces whose work is independent of AAE depot regeneration, so they can race in parallel against ₣A_'s live-infra spine. The intended pattern is a second officium working in `../rbm_beta_recipemuster` while the primary officium continues ₣A_. Cross-officium discipline is strict (file-level blacklists, additive-only commits) and the heat runs fast suite only during the parallel period — crucible/service/complete are reserved for ₣A_.

## Remaining paces (in heat order)

- **₢BAAAA** — `oauth-trust-hygiene-sweep` [rough]. Mechanical multi-step cleanup of ZRBGO/ZRBGP dead constants, OAuth URL constants (F3), behavior-adjacent fixes (F2/F5), comment-only touches, and BCG/producer-reality doc adjustments across rbgo/rbgp/buh/rbgu/rbgg/rbgc/rbrp. Drafted from ₢A_AAN; one notch at the end after tiered shellcheck/fast/credential-lifecycle verification.

- **₢BAAAB** — `bcg-shellcheck-cross-module-discipline` [rough]. Decide whether to promote a single `# shellcheck disable=SC2153` directive to a BCG-level mandated prologue line for all kindled modules (uniform mechanical pattern), then survey/apply and retire AAN's per-file disables. Drafted from ₢A_AAY.

- **₢BAAAC** — `oci-magic-string-extraction` [rough]. Extract repeated OCI Distribution Spec keywords (`manifests`, `blobs`, `tags/list`, `v2`) from ~10 inline sites in `rbfr_FoundryRetriever.sh` / `rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh` into either RBFC tinder constants (Option A) or a URL-builder helper (Option B). Drafted from ₢A_AAM.

- **₢BAAAF** — `imageops-fixtures` [rough]. Add two new service-tier theurge fixtures — `reliquary-lifecycle` (10 cases) and `enshrine-lifecycle` (6 cases) — exercising piecemeal jettison and post-jettison enumeration against live GAR. **Hard-blocked on ₢BAAAG**; `muster_absent` cases assert concrete absence only after the cascade fix lands.

- **₢BAAAG** — `jettison-multi-platform-cascade-fix` [rough]. Extend `rbfl_jettison` to cascade through OCI image indices (probe manifest type, enumerate `.manifests[].digest`, DELETE each child, then DELETE index-by-tag) so multi-platform buildx-pushed packages truly empty out of GAR's `ListPackages`. Adds helper `zrbfc_jettison_index_children` in rbfc; amends RBSIJ spec. Regression-tested by the batch_vouch fixture (currently fails at step 03).

- **₢BAAAH** — `kludge-aware-charge-prereq` [rough]. Mint `RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_{CONJURE,KLUDGE,BIND,GRAFT}` constants, swap four bare literals in `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh`, and add a `zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge` discriminator in `rbob_bottle.sh` so charge-time diagnostics for kludge hallmarks point at `rbw-cK{S,B}` instead of the guaranteed-to-fail summon path.

- **₢BAAAI** — `kludge-honors-enshrine-anchors` [rough]. Close the silent divergence where kludge ignores `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR` (uses upstream base) while conjure honors it (uses GAR-anchored base). Adds `ZRBFC_ENSHRINES_BASE` kindled constant, DRYs conjure's resolution, rewrites kludge's slot loop with local-presence guards and `--pull=never`, with remediations pointing at `rbw-iwe` / `docker pull`. Touches RBSAK and RBSAE specs.

## Recently closed
- **₢BAAAD** — `imageops-broaden` (wrapped 260426): added the `rbw-irr`/`rbw-imr`/`rbw-iwr`/`rbw-iJr`/`rbw-ime`/`rbw-iwe`/`rbw-iJe` tabtarget surface and underlying director ops for reliquary and enshrinement image lifecycle.
- **₢BAAAE** — `imageops-precheck` (wrapped 260426): added ordain-time ref-existence check.

## Noteworthy
- **Dependency chain**: ₢BAAAF is **hard-blocked** on ₢BAAAG (cascade fix must land first so `muster_absent` cases can assert concrete absence). The other five remaining paces are independent of one another.
- **Drafted-from lineage**: ₢BAAAA, ₢BAAAB, ₢BAAAC were drafted from ₣A_ paces (AAN, AAY, AAM) — they are the parallel-safe carve-outs.
- **Cross-officium HARD blacklist** appears repeatedly in ₢BAAAA's docket (theurge Rust files + batch-vouch tabtargets reserved for ₣A_'s ₢A_AAS). Mounting agents must respect these constraints.
- All seven remaining paces carry `[rough]` status — none have been mounted yet.

content.text:
# Groom — ₣BA `rbk-11-mvp-parallel-cleanups`

**Status:** racing, 2 of 9 closed. Sibling to ₣A_ — holds post-AAK work independent of depot regeneration so a second officium (in `../rbm_beta_recipemuster`) can race in parallel. Cross-officium discipline is strict: file-level blacklists, additive-only commits, fast suite only.

**Closed**
- ₢BAAAD `imageops-broaden` — added `rbw-irr/imr/iwr/iJr/ime/iwe/iJe` tabtarget surface for reliquary + enshrinement image lifecycle
- ₢BAAAE `imageops-precheck` — ordain-time ref-existence check

**Remaining (heat order)**
- **₢BAAAA** `oauth-trust-hygiene-sweep` — mechanical sweep of ZRBGO/ZRBGP dead constants, OAuth URL constants, behavior-adjacent fixes, and BCG/producer-reality doc touches across rbgo/rbgp/buh/rbgu/rbgg/rbgc/rbrp. Drafted from ₢A_AAN. One notch at the end after tiered verification.
- **₢BAAAB** `bcg-shellcheck-cross-module-discipline` — decide whether to promote `# shellcheck disable=SC2153` to a BCG-mandated prologue line for all kindled modules, then survey/apply and retire AAN's per-file disables. Drafted from ₢A_AAY.
- **₢BAAAC** `oci-magic-string-extraction` — extract repeated OCI keywords (`manifests`, `blobs`, `tags/list`, `v2`) from ~10 inline sites in rbfr/rbfv into RBFC tinder constants (Option A) or a URL-builder helper (Option B). Drafted from ₢A_AAM.
- **₢BAAAF** `imageops-fixtures` — add two service-tier theurge fixtures: `reliquary-lifecycle` (10 cases) and `enshrine-lifecycle` (6 cases) exercising piecemeal jettison + post-jettison enumeration. **Hard-blocked on ₢BAAAG**.
- **₢BAAAG** `jettison-multi-platform-cascade-fix` — extend `rbfl_jettison` to cascade OCI image indices (probe manifest type, enumerate `.manifests[].digest`, DELETE each child, then DELETE index-by-tag) so multi-platform buildx packages truly empty from GAR's `ListPackages`. Adds `zrbfc_jettison_index_children`, amends RBSIJ. Currently fails batch_vouch step 03.
- **₢BAAAH** `kludge-aware-charge-prereq` — mint `RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_{CONJURE,KLUDGE,BIND,GRAFT}`, swap four bare literals in rbfd, add `zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge` so charge-time diagnostics for kludge hallmarks point at `rbw-cK{S,B}` instead of the guaranteed-failing summon path.
- **₢BAAAI** `kludge-honors-enshrine-anchors` — close the silent divergence where kludge ignores `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR` (upstream base) while conjure honors it (GAR-anchored). Adds `ZRBFC_ENSHRINES_BASE`, DRYs conjure's resolution, rewrites kludge's slot loop with local-presence guards and `--pull=never`. Touches RBSAK + RBSAE.

**Notable**
- ₢BAAAF ⟵ blocked by ₢BAAAG; the other five remaining are independent of one another.
- All seven remaining are `[rough]` — none mounted yet.
- AAA/AAB/AAC are the parallel-safe carve-outs from ₣A_'s AAN/AAY/AAM.

What would you like to do — mount one, refine a docket, or rail the order?

content: Heat ₣BA holds seven parallel-safe cleanup paces racing alongside ₣A_; two are closed, seven remain rough and unmounted. Next action: pick one of AAA/AAB/AAC/AAG/AAH/AAI to mount (AAF is blocked on AAG). (disable recaps in /config)

message.content: pick a safe one to work on now, smaller in scope.

content.text:
**₢BAAAH** `kludge-aware-charge-prereq` — smallest and safest. Pure-additive diagnostics improvement: mint 4 named constants in RBGC, swap 4 bare literals in rbfd, add one `zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge` discriminator in rbob. Three files, no behavior change to the happy path, no spec amendments, no design decision baked in.

Runner-up would be **₢BAAAC** (OCI magic-string extraction) but it requires picking Option A vs Option B first, which adds a design beat.

Mount ₢BAAAH?
